section: Using an Argo CD instance to manage cluster-scoped resources
job_statement: When I need to manage cluster-scoped resources with GitOps, I want to configure my Argo CD instance with appropriate permissions, so I can automate cluster configuration through Git without manual intervention.
related_jobs: Add custom permissions for specific resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize time required to enable cluster-scoped management
Ensure Argo CD has only necessary permissions
Reduce likelihood of permission-related deployment failures
evidence: modules/gitops-using-argo-cd-instance-to-manage-cluster-scoped-resources.adoc, lines 1-51. Main procedure: Edit Subscription to add namespace to ARGOCD_CLUSTER_CONFIG_NAMESPACES environment variable. Verification via UI or CLI.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-using-argo-cd-instance-to-manage-cluster-scoped-resources.adoc (PROCEDURE). This is the foundational configuration step for cluster-level GitOps management.

section: Using an Argo CD instance to manage cluster-scoped resources
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when configuring Argo CD for cluster-scoped management, I want to use the web console to edit the Subscription, so I can visually configure permissions without writing YAML.
parent_job: Configure my Argo CD instance with appropriate permissions
desired_outcomes: Ensure correct namespace is added to environment variable
evidence: modules/gitops-using-argo-cd-instance-to-manage-cluster-scoped-resources.adoc, lines 14-32. UI path: Operators → Installed Operators → GitOps → Subscription → Edit Subscription.
notes: UI-based approach for editing ARGOCD_CLUSTER_CONFIG_NAMESPACES. Alternative to CLI-based editing.

section: Using an Argo CD instance to manage cluster-scoped resources
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when I've configured Argo CD permissions, I want to verify the cluster role assignment, so I can confirm Argo CD can manage cluster-scoped resources before deploying applications.
parent_job: Configure my Argo CD instance with appropriate permissions
desired_outcomes: Reduce likelihood of discovering permission issues during application deployment
Ensure correct cluster role is created
evidence: modules/gitops-using-argo-cd-instance-to-manage-cluster-scoped-resources.adoc, lines 34-51. Verification via UI (User Management → Roles) or CLI (oc auth can-i create oauth).
notes: Confirmation step with two approaches: UI search for argocd-application-controller role, or CLI verification of specific permission.

section: Default permissions of an Argocd instance
job_statement: When planning my Argo CD deployment, I want to understand the default permission model, so I can determine what additional permissions I need to configure.
related_jobs: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to understand permission boundaries
Reduce likelihood of over-permissioning Argo CD
evidence: modules/gitops-default-permissions-of-an-argocd-instance.adoc, lines 1-47. Default: admin privileges in deployment namespace, cluster-wide read on all resources.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-default-permissions-of-an-argocd-instance.adoc (PROCEDURE). Reference content explaining default permission model and customization options.

section: Running the Argo CD instance at the cluster-level
job_statement: When operating a production cluster with infrastructure nodes, I want to run Argo CD on dedicated infrastructure nodes, so I can isolate GitOps workloads from application workloads and ensure predictable performance.
prerequisites: Have infrastructure nodes available or create them
desired_outcomes: Ensure Argo CD workloads don't interfere with application workloads
evidence: modules/go-run-argo-cd-instance-on-infrastructure-nodes.adoc, lines 1-58. Procedure: Label nodes, optionally apply taints, add runOnInfra toggle to GitOpsService CR.
notes: Source module: modules/go-run-argo-cd-instance-on-infrastructure-nodes.adoc (PROCEDURE). Production best practice for workload isolation.

section: Creating an application by using the Argo CD dashboard
job_statement: When I need to deploy cluster configurations via GitOps, I want to create an Argo CD application pointing to my Git repository, so I can manage cluster state declaratively.
prerequisites: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
Have Git repository with cluster configurations
Have namespace labeled for Argo CD management
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to deploy cluster configurations
Ensure Git repository is source of truth
evidence: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc, lines 1-59. Creates cluster-configs application pointing to cluster directory with namespace spring-petclinic.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc (PROCEDURE). Main job for deploying cluster configurations via GitOps.

section: Creating an application by using the Argo CD dashboard
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when creating an Argo CD application, I want to use the web dashboard, so I can configure the application visually without writing YAML manifests.
parent_job: Create an Argo CD application pointing to my Git repository
related_jobs: Label namespace for Argo CD management
desired_outcomes: Complete application creation in under 5 minutes
Ensure correct Git path and directory recursion settings
evidence: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-argo-cd-dashboard.adoc, lines 25-54. Dashboard workflow: NEW APP → configure parameters → CREATE.
notes: UI-based approach. Configuration includes Application Name, Project, Sync Policy, Repository URL, Path, Destination, Namespace, and Directory Recurse option.

section: Creating an application by using the Argo CD dashboard
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, after creating an Argo CD application, I want to label the target namespace for management, so I can ensure the Argo CD instance has permission to deploy resources there.
parent_job: Create an Argo CD application pointing to my Git repository
desired_outcomes: Enable Argo CD to manage namespace resources
notes: Critical post-creation step. Without this label, Argo CD cannot manage resources in the namespace.

section: Creating an application by using the oc tool
job_statement: As a platform engineer, when creating an Argo CD application, I want to use the oc CLI tool, so I can automate application creation and manage it as code.
parent_job: Create an Argo CD application pointing to my Git repository
evidence: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-oc-tool.adoc, lines 1-68. Workflow: Clone repo → oc create -f → oc get application → label namespace.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-creating-an-application-by-using-the-oc-tool.adoc (PROCEDURE). CLI-based approach, alternative to dashboard method.

section: Synchronizing your application with your Git repository
job_statement: When my Argo CD application is created with manual sync policy, I want to trigger synchronization, so I can apply Git repository changes to the cluster in a controlled manner.
desired_outcomes: Minimize time from Git change to cluster deployment
Ensure changes are reviewed before application
Reduce risk of unexpected cluster changes
evidence: modules/gitops-synchronizing-your-application-application-with-your-git-repository.adoc, lines 1-21. Workflow: Application shows Missing/OutOfSync → SYNC → review → SYNCHRONIZE → verify Healthy/Synced.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-synchronizing-your-application-application-with-your-git-repository.adoc (PROCEDURE). Core execution step for applying cluster configurations.

section: Synchronizing your application with your Git repository
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, after synchronizing cluster configurations, I want to verify the changes in the OpenShift console, so I can confirm the configurations were applied correctly.
parent_job: Trigger synchronization to apply Git repository changes
desired_outcomes: Reduce likelihood of undetected configuration issues
evidence: modules/gitops-synchronizing-your-application-application-with-your-git-repository.adoc, lines 14-20. Verification: Check link in console menu, verify namespace exists.
notes: Confirmation workflow validates actual cluster state matches Git repository definitions.

job_statement: When planning cluster configuration via GitOps, I want to understand what cluster-scoped resources Argo CD can manage by default, so I can determine if additional permissions are needed.
related_jobs: Add custom permissions for additional resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to understand permission scope
Ensure only required permissions are granted
evidence: modules/gitops-inbuilt-permissions-for-cluster-config.adoc, lines 1-25. Default permissions include operators.coreos.com, user.openshift.io, rbac, config.openshift.io, storage, console.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-inbuilt-permissions-for-cluster-config.adoc (REFERENCE). Lists specific resource groups Argo CD can manage without additional configuration.

job_statement: When default Argo CD permissions are insufficient, I want to create custom cluster roles and bindings, so I can enable Argo CD to manage additional cluster resources securely.
related_jobs: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to grant additional permissions
evidence: modules/gitops-additional-permissions-for-cluster-config.adoc, lines 1-54. Procedure: Create ClusterRole → Create ClusterRoleBinding linking to openshift-gitops-argocd-application-controller service account.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-additional-permissions-for-cluster-config.adoc (PROCEDURE). Example shows granting secrets management permissions.

job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when granting additional permissions, I want to use the web console to create cluster roles and bindings, so I can configure permissions without writing RBAC YAML.
parent_job: Create custom cluster roles and bindings
desired_outcomes: Complete permission configuration in under 10 minutes
evidence: modules/gitops-additional-permissions-for-cluster-config.adoc, lines 13-37. UI workflow: User Management → Roles → Create Role → Create Binding with ServiceAccount subject.
notes: UI-based approach for RBAC configuration. Requires specifying openshift-gitops namespace and openshift-gitops-argocd-application-controller service account.

job_statement: When managing cluster configuration as code, I want to automate Operator installation via GitOps, so I can eliminate manual Operator installation procedures and ensure consistent Operator deployments across clusters.
prerequisites: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
Have Operator subscription definitions in Git repository
desired_outcomes: Minimize time to install Operators across multiple clusters
evidence: modules/gitops-installing-olm-operators-using-gitops.adoc, lines 1-80. GitOps automatically installs Operators when Subscription resources are placed in Git repository.
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-installing-olm-operators-using-gitops.adoc (PROCEDURE). Core use case for cluster configuration as code.

section: Installing OLM Operators using GitOps - cluster-scoped
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when installing cluster-scoped Operators via GitOps, I want to only manage the Subscription resource in Git, so I can leverage the default global-operators OperatorGroup and simplify my GitOps repository.
prerequisites: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
desired_outcomes: Ensure Operators install in openshift-operators namespace
evidence: modules/gitops-installing-olm-operators-using-gitops.adoc, lines 15-35. Example: Grafana Operator with only Subscription resource needed.
notes: Cluster-scoped Operators use default global-operators OperatorGroup, so only Subscription is required in Git.

section: Installing OLM Operators using GitOps - namespace-scoped
job_statement: As a cluster administrator, when installing namespace-scoped Operators via GitOps, I want to manage both Subscription and OperatorGroup resources in Git, so I can ensure Operators are installed in the correct namespace with proper targeting.
prerequisites: Configure Argo CD for cluster-scoped resources
related_jobs: Avoid multiple OperatorGroups in same namespace
desired_outcomes: Ensure Operator installs in correct namespace
evidence: modules/gitops-installing-olm-operators-using-gitops.adoc, lines 37-79. Example: Ansible Automation Platform with Namespace, OperatorGroup, and Subscription. Warning about TooManyOperatorGroups failure.
notes: Namespace-scoped Operators require both OperatorGroup and Subscription. Critical warning: Only one OperatorGroup per namespace to avoid CSV failures.

section: Installing OLM Operators using GitOps - avoiding conflicts
job_statement: When deploying multiple Operators to the same namespace via GitOps, I want to ensure only one OperatorGroup exists, so I can prevent CSV transition failures and ensure successful Operator installation.
desired_outcomes: Reduce likelihood of CSV failure states
Avoid manual intervention for pending CSVs
evidence: modules/gitops-installing-olm-operators-using-gitops.adoc, lines 76-79. Warning: More than one OperatorGroup causes TooManyOperatorGroups failure, requiring manual approval.
notes: Critical constraint for multi-Operator deployments. Requires careful GitOps repository structure to ensure single OperatorGroup per namespace.
